message:
fix(fj-runtime): harden BufferView::slice against range overflow (frankenjax-xjpu)

BufferView::slice computed offset + len with raw addition before
checking against self.data.len(). With adversarial or saturated
usize inputs that addition overflows — debug builds panic with
"attempt to add with overflow", release builds wrap and the
subsequent self.data[offset..offset + len] slice either panics out
of bounds or, worse, returns a wrapping range that confuses the
caller. Either outcome violates the documented contract that
out-of-range slices return None.

Replace `offset + len > self.data.len()` with
`let end = offset.checked_add(len)?;` followed by `end >
self.data.len()` so any overflow short-circuits to None on the very
first line. Reuse `end` in the slice expression so the bounds check
and the actual slice see the exact same value (no second addition,
no chance of a debug/release split).

Add buffer_view_slice_overflow_returns_none covering both adversarial
shapes documented in the issue:
- offset=usize::MAX, len=1 — overflow on the offset side
- offset=1, len=usize::MAX  — overflow on the len side

Both must return None and must not panic. Test allocates only the
existing 3-byte Buffer, so it stays cheap.

Closes the implementation portion of frankenjax-xjpu (Harden BufferView
slice range overflow). Acceptance gates from the issue body
(rch cargo test/check/clippy on fj-runtime, cargo fmt --check, ubs)
are run by the harness on the next pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
